_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Authorizing Official only: Determine if the security and privacy risk from operating a system or using a system, service, or application from an external provider is acceptable.
reference_document_element_description: Review and approve data access requests, data sharing agreements, and data use authorizations for data assets within the assigned domain.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI DG domain. DG-T-013 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-007 (RC, cosine: 0.412), SP-T-008 (SP, cosine: 0.406).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.6/10 (raw 0.359). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [acceptable, application, authorizing, determine, external, official] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, agreements, approve, assets, assigned, authorizations] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.855 (P=0.854, R=0.857). Balanced P/R — the 27-word DCWF element and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (96.2%). "Authorizing Official only: Determine if the security and privacy risk from opera" and "Review and approve data access requests, data sharing agreements, and data use a" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.419 > STS 0.359 (gap +0.060). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.4%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [acceptable, application, authorizing, determine, external, official]. WIDAI terms: [access, agreements, approve, assets, assigned, authorizations].
